title: Diffusion Models
summary: Forward process: gradually add Gaussian noise until data becomes pure noise ($T$ steps). Reverse process: learn a neural network to predict and remove the noise at each step. Simple MSE loss, stable training, high-quality generation. Powers Stable Diffusion, DALL-E, Midjourney.
key_insights: Forward: $x_t = \sqrt{\bar\alpha_t} \, x_0 + \sqrt{1-\bar\alpha_t} \, \epsilon$. Add noise gradually over $T$ steps. At $t = T$: pure Gaussian noise.
Reverse: network $\epsilon_\theta(x_t, t)$ predicts the noise $\epsilon$ that was added. Simple MSE loss: $L = \|\epsilon - \epsilon_\theta(x_t, t)\|^2$.
Sampling: start from $x_T \sim \mathcal{N}(0, I)$, iteratively denoise: $x_{t-1} = f(x_t, \epsilon_\theta(x_t, t))$.
Training is trivially stable (just MSE regression). No adversarial dynamics, no mode collapse, no posterior collapse.
Quality matches or exceeds GANs. Diversity is natural (different noise samples → different outputs).
The neural network (usually a U-Net or transformer) is conditioned on timestep $t$ — same network for all noise levels.

derivations.title: Why predicting noise works — connection to score matching

steps.name: Score function
steps.math: $\nabla_{x_t} \log q(x_t) = -\frac{\epsilon}{\sqrt{1-\bar\alpha_t}}$. The score (gradient of log-density) points toward data from noise.

steps.name: Predicting noise = estimating score
steps.math: $\epsilon_\theta(x_t, t) \approx -\sqrt{1-\bar\alpha_t} \nabla_{x_t} \log q(x_t)$. The network learns the direction back to clean data.

derivations.consequence: Noise prediction, score matching, and denoising are mathematically equivalent views. The simplicity of MSE loss hides deep theory.

derivations.title: Sampling speed — the main practical limitation

steps.name: Standard DDPM
steps.math: $T = 1000$ steps. Each step = one full network forward pass. Generation is ~1000× slower than GANs.

steps.name: DDIM (fast sampling)
steps.math: Deterministic sampling with larger steps. 50-100 steps instead of 1000. Quality is nearly as good.

steps.name: Latent diffusion (Stable Diffusion)
steps.math: Diffuse in latent space of a VAE, not pixel space. $64 \times 64$ latent instead of $512 \times 512$ pixels. Much faster.

derivations.consequence: Latent diffusion = VAE encoder (compress) + diffusion in latent space (generate) + VAE decoder (upsample). Best of both worlds.

notes: Latent diffusion (Stable Diffusion) = VAE + diffusion + cross-attention conditioning (text→image). Cross-attention from TRANSFORMER.CROSS_ATTENTION directly applies here.
